abstract_zh: 本文介绍了ORPilot，一个开源的智能体式人工智能系统，能够将现实世界中的业务问题转化为可求解的优化模型。与假设问题描述清晰、数据格式规范的学术型LLM-OR工具不同，ORPilot专为生产环境设计：面对模糊的问题描述、大规模原始运营数据，并需在不同求解器后端间实现可移植性。该系统引入了四个创新组件：(1) 会话式访谈智能体，用于获取完整的问题规格；(2) 数据采集智能体，可独立于提示自主检索数据；(3) 参数计算智能体，用于将原始表格数据转换为模型可用的参数；(4) 与求解器无关的中间表示（IR），支持确定性的零LLM调用重编译，适配Gurobi、CPLEX、PuLP、Pyomo或OR-Tools等求解器。此外，系统还采用自纠正重试循环机制，利用求解器的追踪信息进行针对性修复。ORPilot是首个聚焦于生产级真实业务问题而非教科书式运筹学（OR）案例的尝试。在真实问题上的评估表明其表现优异：相较于传统学术基准（IndustryOR、NL4OPT和NLP4LP），ORPilot在IndustryOR基准上准确率超越现有最先进工具，在NL4OPT和NLP4LP基准上也达到了相当水平。

title: SCGNN: Semantic Consistency enhanced Graph Neural Network Guided by Granular-ball Computing
authors: Genhao Tian, Taihua Xu, Shuyin Xia, Qinghua Zhang, Jie Yang, Jianjun Chen
abstract: Capturing semantic consistency among nodes is crucial for effective graph representation learning. Existing approaches typically rely on $k$-nearest neighbors ($k$NN) or other node-level full search algorithms (FSA) to mine semantic relationships via exhaustive pairwise similarity computation, which suffer from high computational complexity and rigid neighbor selection, limiting scalability and introducing noisy connections. In this paper, we propose the Semantic Consistency enhanced Graph Neural Network (SCGNN), a novel plug-and-play framework that leverages granular-ball computing (GBC) to efficiently capture semantic consistency in a scalable manner. Unlike node-level FSA methods, SCGNN models group-level semantic structure by adaptively partitioning nodes into granular balls, significantly reducing computational cost while improving robustness to noise. To effectively utilize the discovered group-level semantic consistency, we design a dual enhancement strategy. Specifically, (1) a structure enhancement module constructs an anchor-based graph structure, where each anchor is a virtual node representing the group-level semantic carried by a granular ball, then injecting group-level semantic information into the graph structure; and (2) a supervision enhancement module performs label consistency checking (LCC) by combining GBC predictions with model-generated pseudo-labels, thereby producing more reliable supervision signals. SCGNN is compatible with various GNN backbones. During the forward propagation of SCGNN, the vanilla graph and the augment graph are jointly encoded, and their predictions are fused; during the backpropagation, the supervision enhancement module provides enhanced supervision signals to guide parameter updates.
title_zh: SCGNN：基于粒球计算的语义一致性增强图神经网络
abstract_zh: 捕捉节点间的语义一致性对于有效的图表示学习至关重要。现有的方法通常依赖于$k$-近邻（$k$NN）或其它基于节点层面的全搜索算法（FSA），通过穷举式的成对相似性计算来挖掘语义关系，但这类方法存在计算复杂度高、邻居选择僵化等问题，限制了可扩展性并引入了噪声连接。本文提出了一种新型即插即用框架——语义一致性增强图神经网络（SCGNN），该框架利用粒度球计算（GBC）以高效且可扩展的方式捕捉语义一致性。与传统的节点级FSA方法不同，SCGNN通过自适应地将节点划分为粒度球，建模群体层面的语义结构，显著降低了计算开销，同时增强了对噪声的鲁棒性。为有效利用所发现的群体级语义一致性，我们设计了一种双重增强策略：（1）结构增强模块构建一种基于锚点的图结构，其中每个锚点是一个虚拟节点，代表一个粒度球所承载的群体级语义，并将该语义信息注入图结构中；（2）监督增强模块通过结合GBC预测结果与模型生成的伪标签，进行标签一致性检查（LCC），从而生成更可靠的监督信号。SCGNN可兼容多种GNN主干网络。在前向传播过程中，原始图与增强图被联合编码，其预测结果进行融合；在反向传播过程中，监督增强模块提供增强后的监督信号，指导参数更新。

title: Beyond State Machines: Executing Network Procedures with Agentic Tool-Calling Sequences
authors: Purna Sai Garigipati, Onur Ayan, Kishor Chandra Joshi, Xueli An
abstract: Agentic AI will be an essential enabling technology for designing future mobile communication systems, which could provide flexible and customized services, automate complex network operations, and drive autonomous decision-making across the network. This work studies how Large Language Model (LLM)-based network AI agents can be utilized to execute network procedures expressed as sequences of tool invocations. We investigate four approaches, which differ in how the agent obtains the procedure and in how execution is distributed between the agent and the underlying tools. We evaluated the latency and execution correctness across these approaches using a User Equipment (UE) IP allocation procedure as a case study. Furthermore, we conduct a stress test to examine how many sequential procedural steps an LLM agent can reliably execute before failure. Our results show that approaches relying on iterative agent-side reasoning incur higher latency and are more prone to execution errors, while approaches where the procedure is encapsulated within a single tool, which internally orchestrates the required steps by invoking other tools, reduce latency by limiting repeated reasoning. The stress-test results further show that the model with advanced tool-calling capability maintains reliable execution over longer procedures than the other evaluated models; however, all models exhibit reliability degradation as procedure length increases, revealing clear execution limits in multi-step tool-based workflows. To systematically analyze failures in procedure execution, we introduce a procedure-specific error taxonomy that categorizes deviations in multi-step procedural execution.
title_zh: 超越状态机：通过代理工具调用序列执行网络程序
abstract_zh: 代理型人工智能（Agentic AI）将成为设计未来移动通信系统的关键使能技术，能够提供灵活且个性化的服务，自动化复杂的网络操作，并推动网络中自主决策的实现。本文研究了基于大语言模型（LLM）的网络AI代理如何通过一系列工具调用序列来执行网络流程。我们探讨了四种不同的方法，这些方法在代理获取流程的方式以及代理与底层工具之间执行任务的分工上存在差异。我们以用户设备（UE）IP地址分配流程为例，评估了这几种方法在延迟和执行正确性方面的表现。此外，我们还进行了压力测试，以考察LLM代理在出现故障前能够可靠执行的连续流程步骤数量。结果表明，依赖迭代式代理端推理的方法具有更高的延迟，并更容易产生执行错误；而将整个流程封装于单一工具中的方法，通过该工具内部调用其他工具来协调所需步骤，可减少重复推理，从而显著降低延迟。压力测试结果进一步显示，具备先进工具调用能力的模型在处理更长流程时仍能保持较高的可靠性，但所有模型在流程长度增加时均表现出可靠性下降的趋势，揭示了多步工具调用工作流中存在的明确执行极限。为系统分析流程执行中的失败情况，我们提出了一种针对具体流程的错误分类体系，用于对多步流程执行中的偏差进行分类。

title: HeavySkill: Heavy Thinking as the Inner Skill in Agentic Harness
authors: Jianing Wang, Linsen Guo, Zhengyu Chen, Qi Guo, Hongyu Zang, Wenjie Shi, Haoxiang Ma, Xiangyu Xi, Xiaoyu Li, Wei Wang, Xunliang Cai
abstract: Recent advances in agentic harness with orchestration frameworks that coordinate multiple agents with memory, skills, and tool use have achieved remarkable success in complex reasoning tasks. However, the underlying mechanism that truly drives performance remains obscured behind intricate system designs. In this paper, we propose HeavySkill, a perspective that views heavy thinking not only as a minimal execution unit in orchestration harness but also as an inner skill internalized within the model's parameters that drives the orchestrator to solve complex tasks. We identify this skill as a two-stage pipeline, i.e., parallel reasoning then summarization, which can operate beneath any agentic harness. We present a systematic empirical study of HeavySkill across diverse domains. Our results show that this inner skill consistently outperforms traditional Best-of-N (BoN) strategies; notably, stronger LLMs can even approach Pass@N performance. Crucially, we demonstrate that the depth and width of heavy thinking, as a learnable skill, can be further scaled via reinforcement learning, offering a promising path toward self-evolving LLMs that internalize complex reasoning without relying on brittle orchestration layers.
title_zh: HeavySkill：作为代理驾驭中内在技能的深度思考
abstract_zh: 近年来，基于协调框架的智能体（agentic）系统在复杂推理任务中取得了显著成功，这些框架能够协调多个具备记忆、技能和工具使用能力的智能体。然而，驱动性能提升的核心机制却隐藏在复杂的系统设计背后，难以被清晰揭示。本文提出“HeavySkill”这一新视角，将深度思考不仅视为协调框架中的最小执行单元，更视为模型参数内部内化的内在技能，该技能能够驱动协调器完成复杂任务。我们识别出这种技能为一个两阶段流程：并行推理后进行总结，该流程可嵌入任意智能体协调框架之下。我们对HeavySkill在多个不同领域进行了系统的实证研究。结果表明，该内在技能持续优于传统的Best-of-N（BoN）策略；尤其值得注意的是，更强的大型语言模型甚至可以接近Pass@N的表现。关键的是，我们证明了深度思考的深度与广度作为可学习的技能，可通过强化学习进一步扩展，为实现无需依赖脆弱协调层的自我演进式大语言模型提供了极具前景的发展路径。

title: CommitSuite: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Commit Classification and Message Generation
authors: Zirui Wan, Zhaonan Wu, Xinyi Hou, Yanjie Zhao, Pengcheng Xia, Haoyu Wang
abstract: High-quality commit messages are critical for maintaining software projects, yet ensuring their consistency and informativeness remains a practical challenge. While the Conventional Commits Specification (CCS) provides a structured format for commit messages, research on CCS-based commit classification and commit message generation (CMG) is limited by the absence of large-scale benchmarks, semantic annotations, and reliable evaluation methods. In this paper, we introduce CommitSuite, a benchmark comprising 63,533 CCS-compliant commits from 243 open-source repositories across seven programming languages. Each commit is labeled with its CCS type and enriched with AST-level code changes, along with LLM-assisted semantic annotations that capture the "what" and "why" behind the change. To evaluate CMG systems, we propose a reference-free framework based on five binary metrics: rationality, comprehensiveness, non-redundancy, authenticity, and logicality, enabling semantic-level assessment without relying on human-written references. Our experiments show that LLMs can effectively support both generation and evaluation, with evaluation achieving 0.849 Cohen's Kappa agreement against human judgments. CommitSuite offers a unified resource for structured commit understanding and facilitates reproducible research on commit classification and generation.
title_zh: CommitSuite：一个全面的提交分类与消息生成基准测试
abstract_zh: 高质量的提交信息对于维护软件项目至关重要，但确保其一致性与信息量仍是一项实际挑战。尽管传统的提交规范（Conventional Commits Specification, CCS）为提交信息提供了结构化格式，但针对基于CCS的提交分类和提交信息生成（CMG）的研究，受限于缺乏大规模基准数据集、语义标注以及可靠的评估方法。本文提出 CommitSuite，一个包含来自7种编程语言、243个开源仓库的63,533条符合CCS规范的提交信息的基准数据集。每条提交均标注了其CCS类型，并通过AST级别的代码变更进行丰富，同时结合大模型辅助的语义标注，捕捉变更的“做什么”和“为什么做”。为评估CMG系统，我们提出一种无需参考文本的评估框架，基于五项二元指标：合理性、完整性、非冗余性、真实性与逻辑性，实现不依赖人工参考文本的语义层面评估。实验表明，大模型在生成与评估任务中均表现优异，评估结果与人工判断之间达到0.849的Cohen's Kappa一致性。CommitSuite为结构化提交理解提供了一个统一资源，推动了提交分类与生成研究的可复现性发展。

title: Zero-Shot Confidence Estimation for Small LLMs: When Supervised Baselines Aren't Worth Training
authors: Luong N. Nguyen
abstract: How reliably can a small language model estimate its own correctness? The answer determines whether local-to-cloud routing-escalating queries a cheap local model cannot handle-can work without supervised training data. As inference costs dominate large language model (LLM) deployment budgets, routing most queries to a cheap local model while reserving expensive cloud calls for hard cases is an increasingly common cost-control strategy. We compare zero-shot confidence signals against RouteLLM-style supervised baselines across three 7-8B model families and two datasets (1,000 and 500 queries per model, respectively). Average token log-probability, which requires no training data, matches or exceeds supervised baselines in-distribution (Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve (AUROC) 0.650-0.714 vs. 0.644-0.676) and substantially outperforms them out-of-distribution (0.717-0.833 vs. 0.512-0.564), because it measures a property of the model's generation rather than the query distribution. This paper further proposes retrieval-conditional self-assessment, a pre-generation signal that selectively injects retrieved knowledge when similarity is high, improving over bare self-assessment by up to +0.069 AUROC at 3-10x lower latency than log-probability. A supervised baseline trained on 1,000 labeled examples never exceeds the zero-shot signal. We release all code, data, and experiment logs.
title_zh: 零样本置信度估计在小型大语言模型中的应用：当有监督基线不值得训练时
abstract_zh:
小型语言模型在多大程度上能够可靠地评估自身答案的正确性？这一问题的答案将决定：无需监督训练数据的情况下，是否可以实现“本地-云端”路由机制——即把本地模型无法处理的复杂查询自动转至云端。由于推理成本主导了大型语言模型（LLM）部署的预算，目前一种日益普遍的成本控制策略是：将大多数查询交由低成本的本地模型处理，仅对困难案例保留昂贵的云端调用。

本研究在三个7-80亿参数规模的模型家族上，对比了零样本（zero-shot）置信度信号与RouteLLM风格的有监督基线方法，使用两个数据集进行测试（每种模型分别包含1,000和500个查询）。结果表明，仅需少量计算、无需任何训练数据的“平均词元对数概率”（average token log-probability）信号，在分布内（in-distribution）的表现与有监督基线相当甚至更优（受试者工作特征曲线下面积AUROC为0.650–0.714 vs. 0.644–0.676），而在分布外（out-of-distribution）场景下则显著超越后者（AUROC达0.717–0.833 vs. 0.512–0.564）。这是因为该指标衡量的是模型生成过程本身的特性，而非查询分布的差异。

本文进一步提出了一种“检索条件下的自我评估”（retrieval-conditional self-assessment）方法：在生成前，当查询与检索到的知识高度相似时，选择性地注入外部知识。该方法相比单纯的自我评估，可提升最高达+0.069的AUROC，且延迟仅为对数概率方法的1/3至1/10。值得注意的是，即使是在1,000个标注样本上训练的有监督基线，其性能也始终未超过零样本信号。

所有代码、数据及实验日志均已公开发布。

title: HEJ-Robust: A Robustness Benchmark for LLM-Based Automated Program Repair
authors: Fazle Rabbi, Jinqiu Yang
abstract: Recent Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown strong performance on automated program repair across standard benchmarks. However, these benchmarks evaluate models on a single canonical form of buggy code and do not reflect the syntactic variations commonly observed in real-world software, leaving robustness largely unexamined. In this work, we construct HEJ-Robust, a robustness benchmark built from HumanEval-Java-Bug using eight semantics-preserving code transformations, resulting in 1,450 transformed instances. We evaluate five fine-tuned LLMs on this benchmark and show that model performance drops by over 50% under several transformations, indicating that current LLM-based repair models lack robustness to minor syntactic variations.
title_zh: HEJ-Robust：基于大语言模型的自动化程序修复鲁棒性基准
abstract_zh: 近期的大语言模型（LLMs）在标准基准测试中表现出色，能够有效实现自动化程序修复。然而，这些基准测试仅针对有缺陷代码的一种标准形式进行评估，未能反映真实软件中常见的语法多样性，导致模型的鲁棒性尚未得到充分考察。在本研究中，我们基于HumanEval-Java-Bug构建了HEJ-Robust这一鲁棒性基准，通过八种语义保持的代码变换生成了1,450个转换后的实例。我们在该基准上评估了五种微调过的LLM，结果表明，在多种代码变换下，模型性能下降超过50%，说明当前基于LLM的修复模型对细微的语法变化缺乏鲁棒性。

title: Beyond Translation Accuracy: Addressing False Failures in LLM-Based Code Translation
authors: Fazle Rabbi, Soumit Kanti Saha, Jinqiu Yang
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in automated code translation. While prior work has focused on improving translation accuracy through advanced prompting and iterative repair, the reliability of the underlying evaluation frameworks has received less attention. In this paper, we demonstrate that a significant number of reported failures in code translation are not due to incorrect logic, but rather evaluation-induced errors stemming from improper compilation flags, missing library links, and unconfigured runtime environments. We conduct a large-scale empirical study across five programming languages (C, C++, Java, Python, Go) and three benchmarks (Avatar, CodeNet, EvalPlus), covering 6,164 translations generated by GPT-4o, DeepSeek-Coder, and Magicoder. Our analysis identifies and categorizes common false negatives, distinguishing pipeline-induced failures that affect any model from model-dependent behaviors that vary across LLMs. Our findings highlight the necessity for transparent, configuration-aware evaluation standards to accurately assess progress in LLM-based code translation.
title_zh: 超越翻译准确性：解决基于大语言模型的代码翻译中的误报问题
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在自动化代码翻译任务中取得了显著成功。尽管以往的研究主要通过先进的提示工程和迭代修复来提升翻译准确性，但对底层评估框架可靠性的关注却相对不足。本文揭示，大量报告中的代码翻译失败并非源于逻辑错误，而是由评估过程引发的错误所致，具体包括不正确的编译标志、缺失的库链接以及未配置的运行时环境。我们针对五种编程语言（C、C++、Java、Python、Go）和三个基准测试集（Avatar、CodeNet、EvalPlus），对 GPT-4o、DeepSeek-Coder 和 Magicoder 生成的 6,164 个代码翻译进行了大规模实证研究。分析结果识别并分类了常见的假阴性案例，区分了影响所有模型的流水线相关故障，以及因模型而异的特定行为。我们的研究强调了建立透明、配置感知的评估标准的重要性，以准确衡量基于大语言模型的代码翻译技术进展。

title: Planner Matters! An Efficient and Unbalanced Multi-agent Collaboration Framework for Long-horizon Planning
authors: Wenyi Wu, Sibo Zhu, Kun Zhou, Biwei Huang
abstract: Language model (LM)-based agents have demonstrated promising capabilities in automating complex tasks from natural language instructions, yet they continue to struggle with long-horizon planning and reasoning. To address this, we propose an enhanced multi-agent framework that decomposes automation into three roles: a planner for high-level decision-making, an actor for task execution, and a memory manager for contextual reasoning. While this modular decomposition aligns with established design patterns, our core contribution lies in a systematic compute-allocation analysis, revealing that planning is the dominant factor influencing task performance. Execution and memory management require significantly less compute and model capacity to achieve competitive results. Building on these insights, we introduce a planner-centric reinforcement learning approach, which exclusively optimizes the planner using trajectory-level rewards from a VLM-as-judge, while freezing the other components. Extensive experiments on benchmarks spanning web navigation, OS control, and tool use demonstrate that concentrating model capacity and learning on high-level planning yields robust and compute-efficient improvements in long-horizon agent automation. Our code is publicly released.
title_zh: 规划者至关重要！一种高效且非均衡的多智能体协作框架用于长时程规划
abstract_zh: 基于语言模型（LM）的智能体在从自然语言指令中自动化复杂任务方面展现了令人瞩目的能力，但仍面临长周期规划与推理方面的挑战。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种增强型多智能体框架，将自动化过程分解为三个角色：负责高层决策的规划者、负责任务执行的执行者，以及负责上下文推理的记忆管理者。尽管这种模块化设计符合已有的系统架构模式，但我们的核心贡献在于一项系统的计算资源分配分析，揭示出规划是影响任务性能的关键因素。相比之下，执行和记忆管理对计算资源及模型容量的需求显著较低，仍可实现具有竞争力的表现。基于这些发现，我们提出一种以规划者为中心的强化学习方法：仅通过视觉-语言模型（VLM）作为评判者提供的轨迹级奖励来优化规划者，同时冻结其他组件。在涵盖网页导航、操作系统控制和工具使用等多个基准任务上的大量实验表明，将模型能力与学习重点集中于高层规划，能够有效提升长周期智能体自动化的鲁棒性与计算效率。相关代码已公开发布。

title: AI-Generated Smells: An Analysis of Code and Architecture in LLM and Agent-Driven Development
authors: Yuecai Zhu, Nikolaos Tsantalis, Peter C. Rigby
abstract: The promise of Large Language Models in automated software engineering is often measured by functional correctness, overlooking the critical issue of long term maintainability. This paper presents a systematic audit of technical debt in AI-generated software, revealing that AI does not eliminate flaws but rather introduces a distinct machine signature of defects. Our multi-scale analysis, spanning single-file algorithmic tasks and complex, agent generated systems, identifies a fundamental Reasoning-Complexity Trade-off: as models become more capable, they generate increasingly bloated and coupled code. This architectural decay is so pronounced that we establish a Volume-Quality Inverse Law, where code volume is a near perfect predictor of structural degradation. Crucially, we demonstrate that neither functional correctness nor detailed prompting mitigates this decay. These findings challenge the current paradigm of prompt-driven generation, reframing the central problem of AI-based software engineering from one of code generation to one of architectural complexity management. We conclude that future progress depends on equipping agents with explicit architectural foresight to ensure the software they build is not just functional, but also maintainable.
title_zh: AI生成的气味：基于大语言模型与智能体驱动开发中的代码与架构分析
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型在自动化软件工程中的潜力，通常以功能正确性来衡量，却忽视了长期可维护性这一关键问题。本文对AI生成软件中的技术债务进行了系统性审计，揭示出AI并未消除缺陷，而是引入了一种独特的机器特征缺陷模式。我们从单文件算法任务到复杂的代理生成系统，开展了多尺度分析，发现了一个根本性的“推理-复杂性权衡”：随着模型能力的提升，其生成的代码变得日益臃肿且耦合度更高。这种架构退化现象极为显著，我们由此确立了“体积-质量反比定律”，即代码体量几乎可以完美预测其结构退化程度。尤为重要的是，我们证明了无论是否具备功能正确性，或是否采用详细提示，都无法缓解这种退化。这些发现挑战了当前以提示驱动生成的范式，将基于AI的软件工程的核心问题，从代码生成重新定义为架构复杂性管理。我们最终得出结论：未来的发展取决于赋予智能体明确的架构前瞻性，以确保它们所构建的软件不仅功能正确，而且真正可维护。

title: SCRIBE: Practical Static Binary Patching via Binary-Aware Recompilation of Decompiled Code
authors: Han Dai, Soumyakant Priyadarshan, Abdullah Imran, Ruoyu Wang, Antonio Bianchi
abstract: When source code or the original toolchain is unavailable, patching binaries is difficult because it requires editing low-level assembly code directly. As an alternative, one can decompile the binary, apply the patch at the source level, and then recompile the modified code. However, as this paper demonstrates, this workflow is hindered by pervasive syntactic and semantic inaccuracies in the output of modern decompilers, many of which prior work has overlooked. To address these challenges, we present SCRIBE, a patching framework that handles syntactic and semantic issues in decompiled code, improving both recompilation success and correctness. SCRIBE's novel "binary-aware" recompilation approach repairs semantic inaccuracies in decompiler output by leveraging information extracted directly from the original binary. In our evaluation, SCRIBE resolved approximately 81% of previously incorrect functions produced by the Hex-Rays decompiler, demonstrating the effectiveness of its approach. Moreover, we show that, using SCRIBE, it is possible to patch 13 of 14 real-world CVEs without access to the original source code and without performing any manual binary editing. To further validate our findings, we conducted a user study with 18 participants. Using SCRIBE, participants achieved 100% patching success, compared to 3.7% without it. Finally, we asked three large language models to generate source-level patches via SCRIBE; all three achieved 100% success when using the framework, demonstrating its potential to enable fully automated patching. Overall, these results indicate that SCRIBE makes source-level patching of binaries accessible and reliable, even without access to the original source.
title_zh: SCRIBE：通过反编译代码的二进制感知重编译实现实用的静态二进制修补
abstract_zh: 当源代码或原始工具链不可用时，修补二进制文件变得十分困难，因为这需要直接编辑低级别的汇编代码。作为一种替代方案，可以将二进制文件反编译为高级源代码，然后在源码级别应用补丁，并重新编译修改后的代码。然而，正如本文所展示的，这一工作流程受到现代反编译器输出中普遍存在且广泛存在的语法和语义错误的严重阻碍，而这些错误在以往的研究中常常被忽视。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了SCRIBE——一种能够处理反编译代码中语法与语义问题的修补框架，显著提升了重新编译的成功率与正确性。SCRIBE采用创新的“二进制感知”重新编译方法，通过直接从原始二进制文件中提取信息，修复反编译结果中的语义不准确之处。在我们的评估中，SCRIBE成功修正了Hex-Rays反编译器此前生成的约81%的错误函数，充分证明了该方法的有效性。此外，我们还展示了，在无需访问原始源代码且不进行任何手动二进制编辑的情况下，使用SCRIBE可成功修补14个真实世界CVE中的13个。为进一步验证研究结论，我们开展了一项包含18名参与者的用户研究：使用SCRIBE的参与者实现了100%的补丁成功率，而未使用SCRIBE的参与者成功率仅为3.7%。最后，我们让三个大型语言模型通过SCRIBE生成源码级补丁，所有三个模型在使用该框架时均达到了100%的成功率，表明SCRIBE具备实现完全自动化补丁的潜力。总体而言，这些结果表明，即使在没有原始源代码的情况下，SCRIBE也能使二进制文件的源码级修补变得可行且可靠。

title: ARIADNE: Agentic Reward-Informed Adaptive Decision Exploration via Blackboard-Driven MCTS for Competitive Program Generation
authors: Minnan Wei, Xiang Chen, Xiaoshuai Niu, Siyu Chen
abstract: Competitive program generation aims to automatically produce correct and efficient solutions for programming-contest problems under strict time and memory constraints. Existing LLM-based approaches often fail to perform explicit algorithmic planning and to handle edge cases robustly, leading to unreliable one-shot generation. Moreover, although execution feedback is essential for iterative debugging and refinement, incorporating such feedback effectively within limited computational budgets remains difficult. To overcome these limitations, we propose {\tool}, a blackboard-driven Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) framework that models program generation as a sequential decision process. {\tool} organizes the generation workflow into five coordinated stages (i.e., strategy selection, code generation, test generation, quality evaluation, and code repair) while maintaining a shared blackboard that accumulates structured evidence to guide subsequent decisions. Experiments on four benchmarks (APPS, CodeContests, CodeContests+, and LiveCodeBench) show that {\tool} consistently achieves the best Pass@1 performance across multiple LLM backends. With GPT-4o, {\tool} attains Pass@1 scores of 41.30, 46.67, 27.27, and 20.91, surpassing the strongest baseline CodeSim by up to 26.06 points, while further improvements are observed with DeepSeek-V3.2. These results indicate that combining global search through MCTS with persistent evidence accumulation on a shared blackboard enables systematic exploration and effective feedback utilization, substantially enhancing the capability of LLMs in competitive program generation.
title_zh: 阿里阿德涅：基于黑板驱动的蒙特卡洛树搜索的智能体奖励感知自适应决策探索，用于竞争性程序生成
abstract_zh: 竞赛程序生成旨在严格的时间和内存约束下，自动生成正确且高效的编程竞赛解决方案。现有的基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法往往缺乏显式的算法规划能力，对边界情况的处理也缺乏鲁棒性，导致一次性生成结果不可靠。此外，尽管执行反馈对于迭代调试与优化至关重要，但在有限的计算预算下有效整合此类反馈仍具挑战性。为克服这些局限，我们提出 {\tool}——一种基于黑板驱动的蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）框架，将程序生成建模为一个序列决策过程。{\tool} 将生成流程组织为五个协同阶段：策略选择、代码生成、测试用例生成、质量评估以及代码修复，并通过一个共享的黑板持续积累结构化证据，以指导后续决策。在四个基准数据集（APPS、CodeContests、CodeContests+ 和 LiveCodeBench）上的实验表明，{\tool} 在多种 LLM 后端上均 consistently 达到了最优的 Pass@1 性能。使用 GPT-4o 时，{\tool} 在上述数据集上分别取得 41.30、46.67、27.27 和 20.91 的 Pass@1 分数，相较于最强基线 CodeSim 最高提升达 26.06 分；而采用 DeepSeek-V3.2 时进一步观察到性能提升。结果表明，结合 MCTS 的全局搜索机制与共享黑板上的持续证据积累，能够实现系统化的探索与有效的反馈利用，显著增强 LLM 在竞赛程序生成任务中的能力。

title: MEMAUDIT: An Exact Package-Oracle Evaluation Protocol for Budgeted Long-Term LLM Memory Writing
authors: Nishant Bhargava, Rodrigo Sobral Barrento
abstract: Long-term LLM agents must compress streams of past interactions into persistent memory before future queries are known. Existing evaluations usually measure final question-answering accuracy, which entangles memory writing with retrieval, prompting, and reader reasoning. We introduce MEMAUDIT, an exact packageoracle evaluation protocol for budgeted long-term memory writing. A MEMAUDIT package fixes an experience stream, candidate memory representations, storage costs, semantic evidence units, future-query requirements, and a budget, turning write-time memory selection into a finite auditable optimization problem with a certified denominator. We instantiate this protocol with a concave-over-modular semantic coverage objective under storage and one-representation-per-experience constraints, and compute exact package optima using branch-and-bound with MILP certification. Across controlled exact packages, validity-heavy stress tests, human-audited natural support slices, and exported Mem0, A-Mem, and Letta stores, MEMAUDIT separates representation quality, validity-state preservation, and budget-aware selection effects that end-to-end QA cannot localize. The resulting artifact provides reusable package generators, certified solvers, natural package exports, external-system scorers, and cached reproducibility metadata for evaluating what memory writers actually preserve under fixed storage budgets.
title_zh: MEMAUDIT：一种用于预算长期大语言模型记忆写入的精确包-Oracle评估协议
abstract_zh: 长期大语言模型代理必须在未知未来查询的情况下，将过往交互的流数据压缩为持久记忆。现有的评估方法通常仅衡量最终问答的准确性，这使得记忆写入、检索、提示生成和阅读推理等环节相互纠缠。我们提出 MEMAUDIT——一种针对有限预算下长期记忆写入的精确包级oracle评估协议。一个 MEMAUDIT 包固定了经验流、候选记忆表征、存储成本、语义证据单元、未来查询需求以及预算，从而将写入时的记忆选择问题转化为一个具有可验证分母的有限审计优化问题，并提供严格证明。我们在此协议中采用凹函数-模态（concave-over-modular）语义覆盖目标，在存储限制和每经验仅保留一表示的约束下，利用分支定界法结合混合整数线性规划（MILP）认证，计算出精确的包级最优解。在受控的精确包、以有效性为重点的压力测试、经人类审核的自然支持片段，以及导出的 Mem0、A-Mem 和 Letta 存储系统中，MEMAUDIT 成功分离出表征质量、有效状态保持能力与预算感知选择效应，而这些因素在端到端问答评估中无法被单独定位。由此产生的成果包括可复用的包生成器、经认证的求解器、自然包导出格式、外部系统评分工具，以及缓存的可复现性元数据，可用于评估在固定存储预算下，记忆写入者实际保留的内容。

title: FANE: FPGA-Based FP8 Approximate Neural Network Engine
authors: Han Bao, Shidi Tang, Jingdong Li, Ahmed Sadaqa, Ruiqi Chen, Ming Ling, Bruno da Silva
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The 8-bit floating-point (FP8) format has gained growing interest in neural networks (NNs) for its superior dynamic range over traditional INT8. However, multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations remain a major source of power consumption during inference of NNs, which makes DSP-free design important, especially for edge FPGAs with few or no DSPs. Therefore, this brief presents FPGA-based FP8 approximate neural network engine (FANE), an FPGA-based approximate NN engine for FP8. We first introduce a novel approximation method that replaces the multiplications by linear additions. This approximate method reduces power consumption while maintaining high accuracy, outperforming the latest FP8 approximate multiplier by 53.15%. Based on this design, we construct an FP8 MAC unit and integrate it into both a convolution engine and a matrix–vector multiplication (MVM) unit. Finally, we integrate our design into a large language model (LLM). The result shows 61.5% higher efficiency (TOPS/W) than the previous design, demonstrating the superiority of FANE in terms of performance and power efficiency. The code of FANE is available on our <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/hanbao04/FANE-FPGA-based-FP8-Approximate-Neural-Network-Engine.git</uri>
title_zh: 基于FPGA的FP8近似神经网络引擎
abstract_zh: 8位浮点数（FP8）格式因其相较于传统INT8更优的动态范围，近年来在神经网络（NNs）中受到越来越多关注。然而，在神经网络推理过程中，乘加（MAC）操作仍是功耗的主要来源，因此对于DSP资源有限或几乎没有DSP的边缘FPGA而言，实现无DSP设计显得尤为重要。为此，本文提出了一种基于FPGA的FP8近似神经网络引擎（FANE），即一种面向FP8的FPGA近似神经网络加速器。我们首先引入一种新颖的近似方法，用线性加法替代乘法运算，该方法在保持高精度的同时显著降低了功耗，相比最新的FP8近似乘法器性能提升达53.15%。基于此设计，我们构建了FP8乘加单元，并将其集成至卷积引擎和矩阵-向量乘法（MVM）单元中。最后，我们将该设计应用于大型语言模型（LLM）。实验结果表明，与先前设计相比，FANE在能效方面提升了61.5%（TOPS/W），充分展示了其在性能与能效方面的优越性。FANE的代码已开源，可通过以下链接获取：https://github.com/hanbao04/FANE-FPGA-based-FP8-Approximate-Neural-Network-Engine.git

title: Embedded Realization of Deep Learning Based LMPC for PMSM Drives
authors: Akash Patil, Yoshit Tapar, Pramod Ubare, Nirlipta Ranjan Mohanty, D. N. Sonawane
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In today's time, Model Predictive Control (MPC) has gained attention as a viable alternative to conventional control approaches in the motor control applications due to its capability to handle multi-input, multi-output constraints. However, due to its optimization-based nature, MPC requires significant computational resources, necessitating strong hardware and robust algorithms when implemented on embedded systems. MPC's computational efficiency can be improved by using better optimization solvers or better MPC design, but each has its own set of limitations. This research suggests deep neural network based linear model predictive control (DNN-MPC) as a method to reduce computational burden while maintaining performance characteristics. Additionally, this study demonstrates a systematic process of embedded implementation on low-end microcontrollers. DNN model is trained using data generated through a Monte Carlo method based on the LMPC model. The DNN-MPC attained an accuracy of 97.28%, acquiring all the characteristics of LMPC. Despite this negligible reduction in accuracy, the DNN-based model offers substantial improvements in computational efficiency, making it a viable alternative for real-time applications. The proposed DNN-MPC approach has validated using hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) co-simulation on a low-end STM-32 microcontroller. Furthermore, this study also presents a systematic approach of Embedded code-generation on embedded hardware.
title_zh: 基于深度学习的LMPC在PMSM驱动中的嵌入式实现
abstract_zh: 在当今时代，由于模型预测控制（MPC）能够有效处理多输入、多输出的约束条件，其在电机控制应用中逐渐成为传统控制方法的一种可行替代方案，受到广泛关注。然而，由于MPC基于优化算法的本质，其对计算资源要求较高，在嵌入式系统上实现时需要强大的硬件支持和稳健的算法。尽管通过采用更高效的优化求解器或改进MPC设计可提升MPC的计算效率，但每种方法均有其固有的局限性。本研究提出一种基于深度神经网络的线性模型预测控制（DNN-MPC）方法，旨在降低计算负担的同时保持原有的性能特征。此外，本研究还展示了一套在低端微控制器上进行嵌入式实现的系统化流程。DNN模型通过基于LMPC模型的蒙特卡洛方法生成的数据进行训练，所获得的DNN-MPC精度达到97.28%，完整保留了LMPC的所有特性。尽管精度略有下降，但基于DNN的模型在计算效率方面实现了显著提升，使其成为实时应用中的可行替代方案。所提出的DNN-MPC方法已在低端STM32微控制器上通过硬件在环（HIL）协同仿真进行了验证。此外，本研究还提出了一套完整的嵌入式代码生成方法，适用于实际嵌入式硬件平台。

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Efficiency in Software Engineering and Banking
authors: Nikhil Teja Gurram
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: AI is changing the landscape of software development and banking, allowing us to work more efficiently, accurately, and quickly by taking advantage of real-time datadriven decision-making. In this paper, I surveyed the AI methods and tools in Software Development methodologies, including Banking. Software Development in line with the accelerated development velocity, AI along the software cycle assists in generating code automatically, predicting anomalies on already implemented code, “predictively” (not preventively) maintaining applications, testing more smartly and to optimize a project management, lowering overall time and rolling costs of development - but still providing more quality. Banking does it in the field of fraud detection and risk analysis, as well as complex data analysis / IP management products, personalized customer service, workflow automation, and quicker implementation speed backed by enhanced security to ensure a better client experience. This paper also explained real-life application areas and effects grounded in the two fields with leading AI technologies, including machine learning (ML), natural language processing (NLP), and robotic process automation (RPA). This paper addresses the struggle related to privacy, ethical concerns, regulation, and provisioned system. AI adoption is employed strategically to achieve not only operational benefits but also innovation and competitive advantage in software engineering and in banking, thereby supporting firms' capability to address continuous technological and business change.
title_zh: 利用人工智能提升软件工程与银行业务的效率
abstract_zh: 人工智能正在改变软件开发与银行业务的格局，通过利用实时数据驱动的决策机制，使我们能够更高效、更准确、更快地开展工作。本文调查了人工智能在软件开发方法学（包括银行业）中的应用方法与工具。随着开发速度的加快，人工智能贯穿整个软件生命周期，协助实现代码自动生成、对已实现代码中的异常进行预测、以“预测性”而非“预防性”的方式维护应用程序、更智能地进行测试，并优化项目管理，从而在降低整体开发时间和成本的同时，仍能提供更高的质量。在银行业，人工智能则应用于欺诈检测与风险分析、复杂数据分析/知识产权管理产品、个性化客户服务、流程自动化，以及在增强安全性的基础上实现更快的系统部署，从而提升客户体验。本文还结合机器学习（ML）、自然语言处理（NLP）和机器人流程自动化（RPA）等前沿人工智能技术，阐述了这两个领域中真实应用场景及其实际效果。此外，本文也探讨了与隐私保护、伦理问题、监管合规及系统可靠性相关的挑战。人工智能的战略性应用不仅带来了运营效益，还推动了软件工程与银行业的创新与竞争优势，从而增强企业应对持续技术变革与商业环境变化的能力。

title: Hybrid-Polarity Logic Design and An Automated Algorithm for Glitch-Optimized Arithmetic Units
authors: Yuxuan Du, Zhangrui Qian, Tuo Li, Changhong Wang, Zhihao Yan, Kaize Zhou, Zhuo Chen, Jun Yin, Yan Lu, Weiwei Shan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) processors and Digital Signal Processing (DSP) chips require high-efficiency arithmetic units, while glitch-induced power has become a major obstacle to further efficiency improvement. To address this, a hybrid-polarity logic design method and an automated algorithm are proposed for low-power and glitch-optimized arithmetic units. The output inverters of full/half adders are removed to eliminate redundant switching and alleviate path imbalance. The logical duality of these inverted cells is exploited to enable both conventional logic (positive polarity) and its inverted form (negative polarity) to coexist and hybrid-propagate in arithmetic units. Then, a glitch-analysis-based inverter re-insertion strategy is used to ensure logic correctness with minimal power cost. The automated algorithm merges partial products from complex computations and explores hybrid-polarity circuit netlists with optimized power efficiency. Simulated in a 28 nm CMOS process, the proposed unsigned and signed multipliers achieve up to 54.40% and 55.92% power gains, 13.67% and 11.43% delay reduction, respectively, compared with their corresponding Design Compiler-generated baselines. When applied to dot-product modules in an AI accelerator, our design achieves up to 57.87% power reduction. The results verify the effectiveness of our hybrid-polarity design in mitigating glitches and enhancing efficiency for large-scale digital systems.
title_zh: 混合极性逻辑设计及一种用于毛刺优化算术单元的自动化算法
abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）处理器和数字信号处理（DSP）芯片需要高效能的算术单元，而毛刺引起的功耗已成为进一步提升能效的主要障碍。为解决这一问题，本文提出一种混合极性逻辑设计方法及自动化算法，以实现低功耗且抗毛刺优化的算术单元。通过移除全加器/半加器的输出反相器，消除冗余翻转并缓解路径不平衡问题。利用这些反相单元的逻辑对偶性，使传统逻辑（正极性）及其反相形式（负极性）能够在算术单元中共存并进行混合传播。随后，采用基于毛刺分析的反相器重新插入策略，在保证逻辑正确性的前提下将功耗成本降至最低。所提出的自动化算法能够合并复杂计算中的部分积，并探索具有优化功耗效率的混合极性电路网表。在28 nm CMOS工艺下仿真验证，所设计的无符号与有符号乘法器相较于Design Compiler生成的基准设计，分别实现了最高达54.40%和55.92%的功耗降低，延迟分别减少13.67%和11.43%。当应用于AI加速器中的点积模块时，本设计可实现高达57.87%的功耗削减。实验结果验证了所提出的混合极性设计在抑制毛刺、提升大规模数字系统能效方面的有效性。

title: Very-Large-Scale Integration Implementation using Multiplier Topologies with BTC Algorithm and Threshold Optimization for Efficient Computation
authors: R Manasa, Ahmad Al-Qerem, B Mouli Chandra, R Govindaraj, G. Karthik, Bharathi Panduri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) refers to the technology of designing and manufacturing integrated circuits that can accommodate thousands to millions of transistors on a single chip. This paper presents a VIMBTE model that achieves very high performance and hardware efficiency by integrating a drastically improved Block Truncation Coding (BTC) algorithm, a radix-4 Booth multiplier, and a highly customizable VLSI platform suitable for real-time multimedia processing. The main breakthroughs are an adaptive threshold optimization method that enhances Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) without adding to the compression cost and the implementation of $4: 2: 2$ subsampling to reduce the data redundancy level and retain the visual quality. The radix-4 Booth multiplier speeds up arithmetic operations and at the same time reduces the hardware complexity. The hardware implemented in VHDL employs open-source EDA tools for RTL analysis, optimization, binding, and verification. The performance analysis consists of normalized delay, power dissipation, delay, power consumption, and delay metrics.
title_zh: 基于BTC算法与阈值优化的乘法器拓扑在超大规模集成电路中的实现及高效计算
abstract_zh: 超大规模集成（VLSI）是指设计和制造可在单个芯片上容纳数千至数百万个晶体管的集成电路的技术。本文提出了一种名为VIMBTE的模型，通过整合一种显著改进的块截断编码（BTC）算法、一种四进制Booth乘法器以及一个高度可定制的VLSI平台，实现了极高的性能与硬件效率，适用于实时多媒体处理。主要突破包括：一种自适应阈值优化方法，在不增加压缩成本的前提下提升了峰值信噪比（PSNR）；以及采用$4:2:2$色度下采样技术，有效降低数据冗余度的同时保持了良好的视觉质量。四进制Booth乘法器加快了算术运算速度，并同时降低了硬件复杂度。使用VHDL实现的硬件采用了开源EDA工具进行RTL级分析、优化、绑定与验证。性能分析涵盖了归一化延迟、功耗损耗、延迟、功耗消耗及延迟等指标。

title: Blockchain-Enabled Architecture for Secure Validation of Medicines and Consumer Products
authors: K. Maithili, S V Hemanth, S.Tejas Reddy, S. Amutha, Krishnaveni N, Minu Inba Shanthini Watson Benjamin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The issue of drugs and the things that people purchase on a daily basis are a huge problem since it inflicts pain on people and ruins the reputation of businesses. It also makes the buyers not trust much. typically the process of making and selling is, however, not very transparent. People can easily counterfeit records or dispose of them. to achieve this we can apply the notion of a computer known as blockchain. This system allows all the concerned parties to confirm whether a drug or other things that people purchase are authentic or not. It operates by record keeping of all that occurred to a product since it is manufactured to when it is sold in a book which no one is allowed to alter once written. A smart contract will record all the events that occur to an item automatically. This implies that the entire life of the item since it was in the factory to the customer is never hidden. each package has its QR code attached to its data in a ledger. As a buyer scans this code with their phone they can view away whether the item is real or not on Ethereum. We have a prototype that is Ethereum based. The contracts of this prototype are in Solidity. We also have a Python module that links the phone application to the blockchain.
title_zh: 基于区块链的药品及消费品安全验证架构
abstract_zh: 药品问题以及人们日常购买的商品所涉及的造假问题，已成为一个巨大的难题，因为它给人们带来痛苦，并损害了企业的声誉。同时，这也导致消费者对购买的商品缺乏信任。然而，目前药品的生产与销售过程往往不够透明，人们很容易伪造或销毁记录。为解决这一问题，我们可以引入一种名为区块链的计算机技术。该系统能够让所有相关方验证某件药品或其他商品是否真实可靠。其运作方式是：将产品从生产到最终售出的全过程信息记录在一个“账本”中，一旦写入便无法被篡改。智能合约会自动记录物品所经历的每一个事件，这意味着从工厂到消费者手中的整个生命周期都完全透明。每个商品包装上都附有一个二维码，其数据被记录在区块链的账本中。当消费者用手机扫描该二维码时，即可通过以太坊平台查看该商品是否为正品。我们已经开发出一个基于以太坊的原型系统，该原型的智能合约使用Solidity编写，同时还配备了一个Python模块，用于将手机应用程序与区块链进行连接。

title: Exploiting GAI Tools in Remote Online Logic Design Labs
authors: Mazen Alkhatib, Yanzhen Qu, Richard Cai, Ricardo Unglaub
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced remote online lab prototype that enables learners to design and experiment with digital circuits realizing minimized logic expressions. Building on our previous work with AND and OR logic gates, we introduce Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI) powered tools to facilitate the minimization process. Learners are provided with a logic expression to minimize before constructing the corresponding circuit. To validate their work, we implement GAI-driven checks using state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs), including OpenAI ChatGPT3.5/4.0, and Meta AI/ Meta Llama 3.1-405B, trained to minimize logic expressions through iterative refinement. In a conducted study, learners built circuits for minimized logic expressions virtually and tested their operation in an on-campus lab using a webcam. Survey results showed that the application is user-friendly, allowing learners to complete the experiment within a reasonable time frame. Notably, the Meta AI tool demonstrated reliability and effectiveness in supporting the minimization of 4-variable logic expressions, where 100% of participating learners successfully completed the expression minimization experiment.
title_zh: 利用生成式人工智能工具在远程在线逻辑设计实验中的应用
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种增强型远程在线实验原型，使学习者能够设计并实验基于最小化逻辑表达式的数字电路。在先前关于AND与OR逻辑门工作的基础上，我们引入了由生成式人工智能（GAI）驱动的工具，以辅助逻辑表达式的化简过程。学习者需在构建相应电路之前，先对给定的逻辑表达式进行化简。为验证其工作成果，我们采用前沿的大语言模型（LLM）实现GAI驱动的检测机制，包括OpenAI的ChatGPT 3.5/4.0以及Meta AI/Meta Llama 3.1-405B，这些模型经过训练，可通过迭代优化来实现逻辑表达式的最小化。在一项实际研究中，学习者在虚拟环境中构建了最小化逻辑表达式的电路，并通过摄像头在校园实验室中测试其运行情况。调查结果显示，该应用界面友好，学习者能够在合理的时间内完成实验。值得注意的是，Meta AI工具在处理四变量逻辑表达式时表现出高度的可靠性与有效性，所有参与学习者均成功完成了表达式化简实验，成功率达到了100%。

title: SMS-EMOA-Driven Multiobjective Design-Technology Co-Optimization for 5-nm Gate-All-Around Transistors
authors: Hongwei Yao, Xinyu Zou, Xingyu Shang, Jianing Zhang, Xiaojin Li, Yang Shen, Ziyu Liu, Bingyi Ye, Yuhang Zhang, Yanling Shi, Yabin Sun
abstract: Due to the complexity of physical effects and second-order effects, optimizing the electrical properties of sub-5-nm devices is exceptionally challenging. This article presents an S-Metric Selection Evolutionary Multiobjective Optimization Algorithm (SMS-EMOA)-driven Design-Technology Co-Optimization (DTCO) framework. By uniquely integrating a lightweight performance prediction surrogate model, it efficiently identifies Pareto fronts under strict manufacturing constraints. This achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$62\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> speedup and reduces peak memory usage from 12GB to ~200 MB compared to direct technology computer-aided design (TCAD). Furthermore, artificial neural network (ANN)-based compact models can be automatically generated from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> data for fast circuit-level verification. Considering back-end interconnect parasitics, DTCO is performed on a layout-aware 97-stage NAND2 ring oscillator. Compared with random baselines, the optimized device achieves up to 34% lower power and 65% higher performance, while delivering a > 25% improvement in SRAM Static Noise Margin (SNM) for robust stability. This framework establishes a tight, scalable connection across process, device, layout, and circuit co-optimization.
title_zh: 基于SMS-EMOA的5纳米环绕栅极晶体管多目标设计-工艺协同优化
abstract_zh: 由于物理效应和二级效应的复杂性，优化5纳米以下器件的电学性能极具挑战性。本文提出了一种基于S-Metric选择进化多目标优化算法（SMS-EMOA）驱动的设计-工艺协同优化（DTCO）框架。该框架通过独特地集成轻量级性能预测代理模型，在严格的制造约束条件下高效识别帕累托前沿。与直接使用技术计算机辅助设计（TCAD）相比，该方法实现了高达62倍的加速，并将峰值内存占用从12GB降低至约200MB。此外，基于人工神经网络（ANN）的紧凑模型可自动从I–V和C–V数据中生成，从而实现快速电路级验证。在考虑后端互连寄生效应的基础上，DTCO在布局感知的97级NAND2环形振荡器上进行了实施。与随机基线方案相比，优化后的器件在功耗降低达34%、性能提升65%的同时，还实现了SRAM静态噪声容限（SNM）超过25%的改善，显著提升了电路稳定性。该框架建立了工艺、器件、版图与电路协同优化之间紧密且可扩展的联系。

title: LLM Assisted SecOps: A Cognitive Assistant Framework for Cybersecurity Analysts
authors: Harsh Dineshbhai Bakori, Nishanth Kumar Pathi, Shinu Abhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The complexity of cyber threats is rapidly outpacing the capacity of human analysts, creating an urgent need for intelligent augmentation in cybersecurity. As cybercriminals employ increasingly sophisticated tactics, traditional methods of threat detection and response are becoming inadequate. This situation necessitates a paradigm shift in how cybersecurity is approached, particularly through the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Large Language Models (LLMs) can be positioned not as autonomous systems but as cognitive assistants that enhance human analysts’ capabilities throughout the cyber kill chain. By translating vast, unstructured data into actionable intelligence, LLMs can provide contextual understanding, enabling analysts to make faster, more informed decisions and transition from a reactive to a proactive defensive strategy. The primary challenge lies in the overwhelming volume of data and the speed at which cyber threats evolve. The objective of this project was to explore the role of LLMs as cognitive assistants in cybersecurity, focusing on their ability to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of human analysts. By leveraging LLMs, we aimed to improve threat identification, incident response, and overall cybersecurity posture, ultimately shifting the focus from merely reacting to threats to anticipating and mitigating them proactively. To achieve these objectives, we employed a comprehensive methodology that involved the collection of diverse datasets, including open-source intelligence, threat reports, and historical incident data. The LLM was connected to perform cybersecurity tasks using an MCP server, enabling it to generate insights and recommendations tailored to the needs of human analysts. The results of our project indicate that the integration of LLMs significantly enhances the capabilities of cybersecurity analysts. By providing real-time analysis and contextual understanding, LLMs enable faster triage of alerts and more accurate threat assessments. Our recommendations include the continued development and integration of LLMs into cybersecurity workflows, emphasizing the importance of human oversight and collaboration.
title_zh: LLM辅助安全运营：面向网络安全分析师的认知助手框架
abstract_zh:
网络威胁的复杂性正迅速超越人类分析师的处理能力，迫切需要在网络安全领域引入智能化辅助手段。随着网络犯罪分子采用日益复杂的攻击手法，传统的威胁检测与响应方法已逐渐显得力不从心。这一现状要求我们对网络安全防护方式实现范式转变，尤其应通过人工智能（AI）技术的深度整合来应对挑战。大型语言模型（LLMs）不应被视为完全自主的系统，而应定位为认知助手，协助人类分析师在整个网络攻击链条中提升决策效率与分析能力。通过将海量、非结构化的数据转化为可操作的情报，LLMs能够提供上下文理解，帮助分析师做出更快速、更精准的判断，从而推动安全防御从被动响应转向主动预防。

当前面临的主要挑战在于数据量庞大以及网络威胁演进速度极快。本项目旨在探索LLMs作为认知助手在网络安全中的作用，重点研究其如何提升人类分析师的工作效率与实战效果。通过运用LLMs，我们致力于优化威胁识别、事件响应及整体安全态势管理，最终实现从“被动应对”向“主动预测与遏制”的战略转型。

为达成上述目标，我们采用了全面的方法论，包括收集多样化的数据集，涵盖开源情报（OSINT）、威胁报告以及历史事件数据等。通过连接MCP服务器，使LLM能够执行具体的网络安全任务，生成符合人类分析师实际需求的洞察与建议。项目结果表明，LLMs的集成显著增强了网络安全分析师的能力：借助实时分析与上下文理解，LLMs有效加快了警报分类速度，提升了威胁评估的准确性。

基于研究成果，我们提出以下建议：持续推进LLMs在网络安全工作流程中的开发与融合应用，同时强调人类监督与人机协同的重要性，确保技术赋能始终以安全、可控和负责任的方式进行。

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Predicting Attack Vulnerabilities in Quantum-Secure Financial Networks
authors: Saurabh Kakkar, Sathiyaviradhan Janarthanan, Bhulakshmi Makkena, Armaan Kakkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In the age of quantum communication and 6G, the safety of financial networks has become a more important concern as cyber threats become more sophisticated. This study examines how deep learning models can be used to improve vulnerability detection and prediction in quantum-secure financial settings. Based on the Quantum-Secure 6G Slicing Dataset, which combines the latest encryption methods, including ECC, AES, and QKD, the study employs preprocessing steps— data cleaning, normalizatio, label encoding, and SMOTE-based balancing to ensure optimal model performance. The suggested ANN was compared with other deep learning and machine learning methods, including Decision Trees (DTs), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), and Logistic Regression (LR). The results of the experiments were found to be the highest with ANN, the accuracy, 92.25, precision, 89.58, recall, 93.99, and F1-score, 91.73, which indicates that the ANN outperforms the existing models. These findings indicate that the ANN will be a powerful and robust method for detecting fraud and forecasting attack vulnerability in quantum-secure financial networks, as it implies that the ANN is an effective way to capture complex nonlinear dependencies and achieve better generalization and predictive stability.
title_zh: 基于深度学习的量子安全金融网络攻击漏洞预测方法
abstract_zh: 在量子通信与6G时代，随着网络攻击手段日益复杂，金融网络的安全性已成为更加重要的议题。本研究探讨了深度学习模型在量子安全金融环境中的漏洞检测与预测能力提升方面的应用。基于融合了最新加密技术（包括ECC、AES和QKD）的量子安全6G切片数据集，研究通过数据清洗、归一化、标签编码以及基于SMOTE的样本平衡等预处理步骤，确保模型达到最佳性能。所提出的ANN模型与其他深度学习及机器学习方法（如决策树DT、卷积神经网络CNN和逻辑回归LR）进行了对比。实验结果表明，ANN表现最优，准确率高达92.25%，精确率为89.58%，召回率为93.99%，F1得分为91.73，充分证明了ANN在现有模型中的优越性。这些发现表明，ANN将成为一种强大且稳健的方法，用于在量子安全金融网络中检测欺诈行为并预测攻击漏洞，因其能够有效捕捉复杂的非线性关系，实现更优的泛化能力和预测稳定性。
